hero.eyebrow: Builder mindset · backend first · full stack direction
hero.title: Hey, I'm Mattia.
hero.subtitle: I build, I learn, I keep growing.
hero.intro: I'm a backend developer with a strong drive toward full stack. I like well-designed systems, good aesthetics, and everything that levels me up.

about.intro: Backend-first developer with 5+ years in .NET, currently at TeamSystem Enterprise. In parallel I'm building Everyday Life Core — a full stack product across React, Flutter and .NET.
about.careerP1: I work on enterprise .NET backend at TeamSystem, developing, maintaining and refactoring complex systems with REST APIs and business logic designed to stay stable, maintainable and scalable over time.
about.careerP2: Alongside work I build Everyday Life Core, a personal full stack project with React, Flutter and .NET that lets me explore realtime, notifications, modern architectures and AI-assisted development.
about.philosophy: "Code should be a system someone else can grow into — not a puzzle they have to solve."
about.card1Desc: I develop and maintain complex .NET systems, REST APIs and business logic for enterprise platforms with hundreds of endpoints.
about.card2Desc: I refactor legacy systems toward more solid architectures, standardize code and introduce unit and integration tests.
about.card3Desc: I'm building a personal project with React, Flutter, PostgreSQL and realtime features to keep growing.
about.quest1Desc: I'm developing a personal SaaS with realtime features, notifications, groups and task management.
about.quest2Desc: Looking for contexts to work on enterprise systems, solid products and quality-driven teams.

skills.bottomDesc: Backend engineer focused on scalable systems, software quality and continuous growth across enterprise and personal products.

projects.intro: A selection of experiences and projects showcasing my work on enterprise .NET backend, legacy modernization and full stack development.
projects.proj1Desc: Development and maintenance of enterprise .NET backend with hundreds of APIs, business-critical logic and a focus on stability and maintainability.
projects.proj2Desc: Progressive refactoring of legacy systems toward Clean Architecture with shared standards, unit tests and integration tests.
projects.proj3Desc: Browser-based real-time cognitive analysis tool via webcam. WebAssembly face tracking (MediaPipe, 468 landmarks/frame), 6 cognitive states, live metrics dashboard and commentary engine with 90 phrases across 3 tones. Zero upload, fully client-side.
projects.proj4Desc: Desktop client-server application for managing One Piece TCG collections, with ASP.NET Core 8 backend, PostgreSQL persistence and CardMarket-ready workflow.
projects.proj5Desc: Personal full stack project: .NET backend, React frontend, Flutter mobile and realtime features for tasks, groups, notifications and daily life management.
projects.log1Note: APIs and business logic on high-load systems.
projects.log2Note: Introduction of unit and integration tests on existing projects.
projects.log3Note: Refactoring toward Clean Architecture and backend standardization.
projects.log4Note: Backend with C#, Python and PHP on portals and data systems.
projects.log5Note: First production backend experience at a software house.

projectNotes.notFoundDesc: No project found for this slug. Go back to the projects section to continue.
projectNotes.ts_headline: High-load enterprise .NET backend with focus on stability, quality and maintainability.
projectNotes.ts_context: Enterprise context with mission-critical APIs, cross-team integrations and an evolving backlog on code already in production.
projectNotes.ts_challenge: Maintain delivery speed without losing control over regressions, domain consistency and technical debt.
projectNotes.ts_s1p1: Maintained and developed endpoints across different business domains, with attention to backward compatibility.
projectNotes.ts_s1p2: Targeted refactoring of high-complexity components to reduce coupling and improve readability.
projectNotes.ts_s1p3: Defined shared guidelines on naming, error handling and application-level validations.
projectNotes.ts_s2p1: Standardized API contracts and error code handling to reduce ambiguity on the consumer side.
projectNotes.ts_s2p2: Incremental test introduction on high-risk areas instead of big-bang testing.
projectNotes.ts_s2p3: Cleaner separation between business logic and infrastructure layer in refactored areas.
projectNotes.ts_s3p1: Aligned with product and QA teams on acceptance criteria and edge cases.
projectNotes.ts_s3p2: Supported production issue debugging with a data-driven approach using logs and application tracing.
projectNotes.ts_s3p3: Contributed to tech reviews to maintain architectural consistency over time.
projectNotes.ts_o1: Estimated reduction of average bug analysis time on refactored modules (~20-30%).
projectNotes.ts_o2: More predictable releases thanks to incremental test coverage on critical flows.
projectNotes.ts_o3: Code that's easier to extend when new business rules arise.
projectNotes.ts_n1: Increase integration test coverage on multi-service flows.
projectNotes.ts_n2: Evolve observability with more granular operational dashboards.
projectNotes.leg_headline: Modernizing legacy systems toward Clean Architecture without blocking business operations.
projectNotes.leg_context: Legacy codebase with high coupling, monolithic classes and limited test automation.
projectNotes.leg_challenge: Reduce technical debt while maintaining operational continuity and compatibility with existing processes.
projectNotes.leg_tl3: Hardening with tests and quality gates
projectNotes.leg_s1p1: Progressive decomposition of god classes into smaller, testable components.
projectNotes.leg_s1p2: Introduction of clear boundaries between Domain, Application, Infrastructure and Presentation.
projectNotes.leg_s1p3: Tranche-based migration with fallback plan to minimize release risk.
projectNotes.leg_s2p1: Adoption of unit and integration tests in areas with the most historical regressions.
projectNotes.leg_s2p2: Refactoring-specific review checklists (dependencies, naming, side effects).
projectNotes.leg_s2p3: Quality gates in the pipeline to prevent structural regressions.
projectNotes.leg_s3p1: Documentation of new conventions to make the refactoring replicable.
projectNotes.leg_s3p2: Supported the team in moving from a monolithic approach to better-distributed responsibilities.
projectNotes.leg_s3p3: Reduced friction between corrective maintenance and evolutionary development.
projectNotes.leg_o1: Credible reduction of cyclomatic complexity in migrated areas.
projectNotes.leg_o2: Better confidence in releases thanks to a more stable test suite.
projectNotes.leg_o3: Healthier architectural foundation for future evolutions.
projectNotes.leg_n1: Extend migration patterns to lower-priority modules.
projectNotes.leg_n2: Increase automation of static and architectural quality checks.
projectNotes.opcm_headline: Desktop client-server product for TCG collectors with ASP.NET Core backend and real-world workflows.
projectNotes.opcm_context: Personal project structured as a real product: REST backend, desktop client, cloud PostgreSQL persistence.
projectNotes.opcm_challenge: Balance desktop UX, API robustness and data modelling useful for advanced filters and real use cases.
projectNotes.opcm_s1p1: API design for card management, expansions, multiple filters and safe update operations.
projectNotes.opcm_s1p2: Service Layer with business rules (logical duplicates, restore, soft-delete).
projectNotes.opcm_s1p3: DTO + AutoMapper to separate API contract from internal data model.
projectNotes.opcm_s2p1: Generic repository pattern for consistent and reusable data access.
projectNotes.opcm_s2p2: Application validations on critical combinations (expansion, number, rarity, condition).
projectNotes.opcm_s2p3: Real workflow support: quick CardMarket status toggle, multiple sorting, free-text search.
projectNotes.opcm_s3p1: Dark/green interface with operational DataGrid, inline actions and contextual feedback.
projectNotes.opcm_s3p2: HTTP Gateway separation on the frontend to keep forms clean and maintainable.
projectNotes.opcm_s3p3: Installer packaging for direct use in Windows environment.
projectNotes.opcm_o1: Application usable end-to-end for real collection management scenarios.
projectNotes.opcm_o2: Clear architecture that facilitates evolutions (dashboard, deck management, price integration).
projectNotes.opcm_o3: Portfolio project demonstrating full ownership from domain to release.
projectNotes.opcm_n1: Direct CardMarket API integration for automatic price updates.
projectNotes.opcm_n2: Frontend evolution toward a modern cross-platform stack.
projectNotes.ns_headline: Real-time cognitive analysis via webcam: WebAssembly face tracking, 6 mental states, live metrics dashboard and commentary engine. Fully client-side, zero upload.
projectNotes.ns_context: Experimental personal project: explore what can be done with computer vision in the browser without server infrastructure. MediaPipe processes 468 facial landmarks per frame via WebAssembly directly on the user's device.
projectNotes.ns_challenge: Translate raw facial signals (blink rate, gaze drift, micro-movements) into cognitive metrics perceived as credible, with a UX that communicates playfulness without being trivial.
projectNotes.ns_tl1: MediaPipe Face Landmarker integration with rolling averages for signal smoothing
projectNotes.ns_tl2: 6-state machine with transition logic and anti-flicker debouncing
projectNotes.ns_tl3: Commentary engine: 90 phrases across 18 pools, 3 tones (Roast / Coach / Corporate), anti-repetition
projectNotes.ns_tl4: Recharts dashboard + session summary with badges and state timeline
projectNotes.ns_tl5: Deploy on Vercel, WASM bundle optimization and cold start
projectNotes.ns_s1p1: MediaPipe Face Landmarker processes 468 facial landmarks per frame via WebAssembly: zero server latency, zero video upload.
projectNotes.ns_s1p2: Rolling average over configurable time windows for smoothing of Focus Score, Gaze Stability, Motion Level and Fatigue Signal.
projectNotes.ns_s1p3: Anti-flicker: state transitions blocked by a debounce timer to prevent flickering on noisy signals.
projectNotes.ns_s1p4: Explicit cleanup of media tracks and animation frames on component dismount to prevent memory leaks.
projectNotes.ns_s2p1: 6 states: Locked In, Focused, Distracted, Tired, Confused Genius, Calibrating — with transition logic based on combined thresholds of the 4 metrics.
projectNotes.ns_s2p2: Calibrating state during initial warm-up: prevents premature classifications in the first few seconds.
projectNotes.ns_s2p3: Each state has different weight in the session summary: the system calculates the dominant session state for the final verdict.
projectNotes.ns_s3p1: 90 hand-written phrases distributed across 18 thematic pools (6 states × 3 tones).
projectNotes.ns_s3p2: Anti-repetition: tracking of last N phrases per state to avoid immediate loops.
projectNotes.ns_s3p3: User-selectable tones: Roast (ironic), Coach (motivational), Corporate (very formal).
projectNotes.ns_s3p4: Rate limiting on comment delivery to avoid overwhelming the user during long sessions.
projectNotes.ns_s4p1: Next.js 15 App Router with React Server components where possible, client components only where needed (webcam, Recharts, Framer Motion).
projectNotes.ns_s4p2: shadcn/ui as base component system: accessibility and consistency without custom design overhead.
projectNotes.ns_s4p3: Framer Motion for cognitive state transitions and visual feedback on metrics.
projectNotes.ns_s4p4: Recharts for live metrics chart with real-time updates without heavy re-renders.
projectNotes.ns_o1: Fully working tool deployed on Vercel: open, calibrate, session, summary.
projectNotes.ns_o2: Stable face tracking at 30fps on consumer hardware without visible UI degradation.
projectNotes.ns_o3: Commentary engine with 90 phrases perceived as varied and non-repetitive in test sessions.
projectNotes.ns_o4: Zero server-side dependencies for tracking: privacy by design, no video data leaves the device.
projectNotes.ns_n1: Session persistence: save session history locally for longitudinal trends.
projectNotes.ns_n2: Personalized calibration: individual baseline to reduce false negatives for users with different facial characteristics.
projectNotes.ns_n3: Session export: PDF or JSON report of aggregated metrics.
projectNotes.ns_n4: Multi-session mode: compare focus scores across different sessions.

everydayLife.archApiDesc: REST API · WebSocket Presence · Health · Metrics
everydayLife.heroDesc: ASP.NET Core 8 backend + Flutter app for collaborative daily life management. Tasks, shared expenses, lists, push notifications, real-time presence and more — all on self-hosted infrastructure.
everydayLife.contextP1: Personal project born from a concrete need: coordinate shared living with flatmates without relying on fragmented generic apps. The primary target is a group of 4 people (1 developer + 3 flatmates), but the system is designed for any group sharing spaces or responsibilities.
everydayLife.contextP2: Backend in production on Ubuntu VPS with Cloudflare Tunnel, Android app in active use. The project is treated as a real product with attention to code quality, security and future scalability.
everydayLife.challengeIntro: Building a complete collaborative system — alone — balancing scope, quality and delivery speed. Main technical challenges:
everydayLife.challengeL1: Real-time presence with scoped subscriptions without degrading performance
everydayLife.challengeL2: Recurring tasks with advanced configuration (JSON config full_custom)
everydayLife.challengeL3: Multi-channel notification system with deduplication and delivery tracking
everydayLife.challengeL4: OTA update with SHA256 validation and in-app installation on Android
everydayLife.challengeL5: Reliable hosting with minimal-cost infrastructure (Ubuntu VPS)
everydayLife.mod1Desc: Registration, email/Google login, email verification, password reset, profile with avatar and username.
everydayLife.mod2Desc: Group CRUD with hierarchical roles (Owner/Admin/Member/Guest), QR invite, join request with approval.
everydayLife.mod2Highlight: Join via deep link + QR code
everydayLife.mod3Desc: Tasks and lists with priority, deadlines, multi-member assignments and advanced recurrences (JSON config).
everydayLife.mod4Desc: Shared lists with categories (sectors), items with quantity/unit, real-time check, custom order.
everydayLife.mod5Desc: Expense recording, automatic fair split, reimbursement tracking, who-owes-whom balances.
everydayLife.mod6Desc: Search by email/username, pending requests, accept/reject, user blocking, complete states.
everydayLife.mod7Desc: Multi-channel FCM push, in-app inbox (7 days), deduplication, delivery tracking, tap routing.
everydayLife.mod8Desc: Persistent WebSocket with scoped subscriptions (global/group/friends), snapshot + delta, auto-reconnect.
everydayLife.mod9Desc: High-precision GPS save, OSM/MapLibre map view, parking history.
everydayLife.mod10Desc: Personal timers with name, type, duration, start/end tracking. Useful for laundry, medication, oven.
everydayLife.mod11Desc: Version check on startup, APK download from backend, SHA256 validation, in-app install. CRITICAL/FORCE support.
everydayLife.eng1L1: Backend on Ubuntu VPS with Cloudflare Tunnel for free public HTTPS.
everydayLife.eng1L2: PostgreSQL in Docker container: isolation, portability, simplified backups.
everydayLife.eng1L3: No cloud provider for backend: full control over data and deployments.
everydayLife.eng1L4: Nginx as reverse proxy with header management and rate limiting.
everydayLife.eng2L1: Persistent WebSocket authenticated via Firebase token.
everydayLife.eng2L2: Scoped subscriptions: each client subscribes only to relevant contexts (global/group/friends/user list).
everydayLife.eng2L3: Initial snapshot + delta updates to minimize payload.
everydayLife.eng2L4: Automatic reconnect with exponential backoff on disconnection.
everydayLife.eng2L5: Presence state synchronized to app lifecycle (foreground/background/terminate).
everydayLife.eng3L1: Simple recurrences: daily, weekly, monthly, yearly — UI configuration.
everydayLife.eng3L2: Advanced recurrences (full_custom): arbitrary JSON configuration for complex patterns.
everydayLife.eng3L3: Backend calculates future occurrences when the task is saved.
everydayLife.eng3L4: When an occurrence expires, the next one is automatically generated.
everydayLife.eng3L5: Automatic push notification to assignees on every recurring state change.
everydayLife.eng4L1: Backend returns latest GitHub release versions: serves the APK via Cloudflare with SHA256 checksum.
everydayLife.eng4L2: App verifies the checksum locally before starting the installation.
everydayLife.eng4L3: Support for critical updates (CRITICAL) with mandatory dialog and forced updates (FORCE UPDATE).
everydayLife.eng4L4: Progress bar during download with error handling and retry.
everydayLife.eng4L5: Admin backend can create GitHub releases via authenticated endpoint.
everydayLife.eng5L1: flutter_riverpod for declarative and testable state management.
everydayLife.eng5L2: Separate providers for each domain (auth, groups, tasks, notifications, presence).
everydayLife.eng5L3: AsyncNotifierProvider for async states with granular loading/error/data.
everydayLife.eng5L4: flutter_secure_storage with AES-GCM for tokens and sensitive preferences.
everydayLife.eng6L1: Firebase Authentication for token management: eliminates custom auth complexity.
everydayLife.eng6L2: Backend validates every request via Firebase Admin SDK (ID token verification).
everydayLife.eng6L3: User sync on first login: backend creates profile and default lists.
everydayLife.eng6L4: Hierarchical group-level roles verified backend-side on every sensitive endpoint.
everydayLife.eng6L5: Deep link with server-side validated invite code to prevent unauthorized joins.
everydayLife.bl1: Shared Expenses UI — creation screen, list, balances
everydayLife.bl2: Timer UI — timer creation and expiry notification
everydayLife.bl3: Background Jobs (Hangfire) — scheduled notifications for task deadlines
everydayLife.bl4: Full homescreen widget — new task, parking, today counter
everydayLife.bl5: Refine Shopping UI — shopping list UX
everydayLife.bl7: Habit Tracking — daily habits with streak
everydayLife.bl8: Bills/Subscriptions reminder — recurring deadlines with alerts
everydayLife.bl9: Extended profile — avatar upload, bio, personal statistics
everydayLife.bl10: iOS Support — Firebase iOS production configuration
everydayLife.bl11: Redis Cache — presence directory and high-volume notifications
everydayLife.bl12: React Web App — browser frontend (planned in roadmap)
everydayLife.bl13: Analytics & Insights — completed tasks, monthly expenses, statistics
everydayLife.oc1: Backend in production on stable self-hosted infrastructure, in daily use.
everydayLife.oc2: Android app working end-to-end for the primary group of 4 people.
everydayLife.oc3: Real-time system (WebSocket presence) fully operational with no additional cloud dependencies.
everydayLife.oc4: OTA update system: updates distributed directly from the app, without a store.
everydayLife.oc5: 11 functional modules, 7 of which fully implemented on both backend and mobile.
everydayLife.oc6: Architecture scalable toward iOS, Web App and additional features without structural refactoring.
everydayLife.lrn1: Designing real-time systems with WebSocket in ASP.NET Core with distributed state management.
everydayLife.lrn2: Production-grade self-hosting on limited hardware with Cloudflare Tunnel.
everydayLife.lrn3: Flutter mobile development with Riverpod: reactive state management on complex domains.
everydayLife.lrn4: Balancing scope and quality alone: knowing when to iterate fast and when to invest in robustness.
